stimulus.content: A group of citizens is unhappy about a new highway being built through their neighborhood. They organize a peaceful march to the state capitol building. They carry signs and ask the governor to stop the project.

stem: Which right allows these citizens to march and share their views with the state government?

choices.text: The right to a speedy trial
The right to bear arms safely
The right to a fair jury vote

answer_explanation: The right to peaceful assembly allows citizens to gather and protest. This is one way people take part in state government. A speedy trial and jury are court rights. The right to bear arms is about weapons, not protests.
